module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 7: Freight Invoice Mismatch & Freight Settlement

learningObjectives.title: Validate freight settlement charges
learningObjectives.description: Compare carrier invoices with planned transportation charges and approvals.

learningObjectives.title: Investigate invoice discrepancies
learningObjectives.description: Use execution events and evidence documents to identify unsupported claims.

learningObjectives.title: Approve only legitimate payments
learningObjectives.description: Apply settlement controls to prevent overpayment and financial leakage.

learningObjectives.title: Document carrier dispute resolution
learningObjectives.description: Record findings and request corrected invoices with proper justification.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 7: Freight Invoice Mismatch & Freight Settlement

sections.content: The shipment has been successfully delivered to Reliance Digital. Proof of Delivery (POD) has been received and the transportation process has entered the Freight Settlement phase. However, during invoice verification, the Finance team identifies a discrepancy between the planned transportation charges and the carrier's invoice.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are the SAP TM Freight Settlement Consultant responsible for validating transportation charges, identifying invoice discrepancies and ensuring that only accurate payments are made to the carrier.

Blue Dart submits an invoice after successful delivery. The carrier claims additional charges for truck detention, emergency vehicle replacement and overnight parking. Finance refuses to approve the invoice because the billed amount is significantly higher than the amount calculated during transportation planning.

The carrier states that the detention charges resulted from the customer's last-minute delivery postponement in Lab 6. However, the Transportation Manager argues that the shipment was unloaded into a temporary warehouse specifically to avoid truck detention.

sections.title: Charge Validation
rows: Truck was released; no detention occurred.

sections.content: Reject the unsupported detention and parking charges. Approve payment only for the agreed transportation and replacement vehicle costs. Document the invoice discrepancy, communicate the findings to the carrier and request a corrected invoice. Update the Freight Settlement Document to reflect the approved payment amount.

sections.title: Consultant Best Practice
sections.content: Never approve a carrier invoice based solely on the submitted amount. Always compare it against SAP TM charge calculations, transportation events, approvals and supporting documents before settlement.

sections.title: Real Production Incident
sections.content: A global retail company discovered that a carrier had repeatedly billed unauthorized detention charges. By comparing SAP TM execution events with freight invoices, the company recovered over INR 1.2 Crores in incorrect transportation payments during a single financial year.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario Question
sections.content: A carrier invoice is INR 33,000 higher than the planned transportation cost. How would you investigate? A consultant should review Freight Orders, execution events, charge calculation profiles, approvals, POD, settlement documents and supporting evidence before approving or rejecting the invoice.

items: Project continues to Lab 8: Transportation Performance KPIs & Management Dashboard.
